text.title: fine-tuning-openai-sft Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for fine-tuning-openai-sft as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using fewer than 50 training examples for SFT.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: With fewer than 50 examples the model memorizes rather than generalizes. A JSONL file with 10% noisy examples degrades the entire run. Use few-shot prompting until sufficient data is collected.

failure-mode.description: Omitting the validation file to save cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a validation file, overfitting is invisible. Epoch miscalibration is the most common failure mode. The validation file cost is negligible compared to the cost of a degraded production model.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding the fine-tuned model ID in application code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAI fine-tuned model IDs look like ft:gpt-4.1-mini-2025-01-01:org::xxxxx. Store in config alongside training data hash in version control. Never hardcode — retraining produces a new ID.
